shared_data_dependencies.description: Other copybooks used by affected programs - potential cross-copybook structural dependencies

copybooks.risk: HIGH - Widely shared (3 programs); ACCOUNT-MGR uses it alongside PAYMENT-RECORD - may have business logic dependencies
MEDIUM - Used by ACCOUNT-MGR which also uses PAYMENT-RECORD; likely has business logic coupling (e.g., payment amount from account balance)

shared_data_dependencies.cross_copybook_contamination: HIGH - ACCOUNT-MGR uses all 3 copybooks together (CUSTOMER-RECORD, ACCOUNT-RECORD, PAYMENT-RECORD); business logic likely couples data from multiple copybooks; changes to PAYMENT-RECORD might require coordinated updates to maintain data integrity

field_mapping_analysis.description: Known field mappings between copybooks in ACCOUNT-MGR.4000-PROCESS-PAYMENT
field_mapping_analysis.risk: CRITICAL - Direct field-to-field mappings exist; any PIC clause or USAGE changes to PAYMENT-RECORD fields may cause data truncation or type mismatch errors

data_items.name: PAY-TRANS-ID
data_items.usage_notes: Transaction identifier - likely used for logging and audit trail; changing length affects file layouts

data_items.name: PAY-CUST-ID
data_items.usage_notes: Customer identifier - populated from CUSTOMER-RECORD; must match CUST-ID PIC clause for data integrity

data_items.name: PAY-ACCT-ID
data_items.usage_notes: Account identifier - populated from ACCOUNT-RECORD; must match ACCT-ID PIC clause

data_items.name: PAY-AMOUNT
data_items.usage_notes: Monetary amount in packed decimal format; used in calculations (e.g., refund threshold check > 1000); populated from ACCT-BALANCE; any precision/scale changes could cause financial errors or data loss

data_items.name: PAY-TYPE
data_items.usage_notes: Payment type field with 88-level conditions (PAY-REGULAR, PAY-REFUND, PAY-REVERSAL); controls EVALUATE statement flow in PAYMENT-HANDLER; changing length or values breaks routing logic

data_items.name: PAY-STATUS
data_items.usage_notes: Payment status field with 88-level conditions (PAY-APPROVED, PAY-PENDING, PAY-REVERSED, PAY-REJECTED); used for status updates; changing length or values affects status tracking

data_items.name: PAY-TIMESTAMP
data_items.usage_notes: Timestamp field populated by FUNCTION CURRENT-DATE; used for audit logging; length must match date function output

condition_names_88.notes: Used in EVALUATE PAY-TYPE in PAYMENT-HANDLER.0000-MAIN; controls which payment processing paragraph is executed
Used for status tracking and potentially in conditional logic; must maintain exact value strings including trailing spaces

critical_considerations.description: COMP-3 packed decimal field (S9(9)V99) - any precision/scale changes could cause financial errors; used in threshold checks (> 1000) and populated from ACCT-BALANCE; ensure decimal alignment
PAY-TYPE field with 88-level conditions controls EVALUATE statement flow in PAYMENT-HANDLER; hardcoded values with trailing spaces ('REGULAR   '); any changes break routing logic
Fields written to PAYMENT-LOG sequential file; historical audit concerns if field layout changes; may require file conversion utilities
ACCOUNT-MGR populates PAYMENT-RECORD fields from CUSTOMER-RECORD and ACCOUNT-RECORD; field PIC clauses must remain compatible for MOVE statements
PAYMENT-RECORD used as interface contract in PAYMENT-HANDLER's LINKAGE SECTION; any structural changes require recompilation of both ACCOUNT-MGR and PAYMENT-HANDLER

file_io_impact.description: Files accessed by affected programs - data integrity and audit concerns

accessed_by.fields_written: All PAYMENT-RECORD fields formatted into WS-LOG-RECORD

files.risk_rationale: Audit log - PAYMENT-RECORD changes affect log format; historical data compatibility concerns; may need file conversion utilities or parallel logging during transition
ACCOUNT-RECORD data (ACCT-BALANCE) mapped to PAYMENT-RECORD (PAY-AMOUNT); ensure data type compatibility maintained
Upstream file - customer data flows to PAYMENT-RECORD via ACCOUNT-MGR; field compatibility checks needed
Entry point file - indirect relationship to PAYMENT-RECORD
Batch statistics report - may need updates if payment data included in reporting

recommended_change_sequence.description: Create detailed field-by-field usage map showing which programs read/write each field; document all 88-level condition name values; capture current PAYMENT-LOG file format for historical reference
recommended_change_sequence.validation: Review by lead developer and DBA; confirm all field mappings documented

recommended_change_sequence.description: Eliminate 2x GOTO statements (lines 50, 58) and replace with structured IF-ELSE or PERFORM logic; this reduces control flow risk before making structural changes to PAYMENT-RECORD
recommended_change_sequence.validation: Unit test PAYMENT-HANDLER; verify all payment types (REGULAR, REFUND, REVERSAL) still processed correctly; confirm return codes unchanged

recommended_change_sequence.description: Implement the structural changes to PAYMENT-RECORD.cpy; if adding fields, append them; if changing PIC clauses, ensure backward compatibility or plan coordinated deployment
recommended_change_sequence.validation: Syntax check; review by data architect; confirm no unintended data truncation or type mismatches

recommended_change_sequence.description: Modify PAYMENT-HANDLER to work with new PAYMENT-RECORD structure; update 2000-VALIDATE-PAYMENT, 4000-LOG-TRANSACTION paragraphs; update WS-LOG-RECORD format if needed
recommended_change_sequence.validation: Unit test all paragraphs; test all payment types; verify PAYMENT-LOG writes correctly; test GOTO elimination still works

recommended_change_sequence.description: Modify ACCOUNT-MGR 4000-PROCESS-PAYMENT paragraph to populate new/changed PAYMENT-RECORD fields; ensure field mappings from CUSTOMER-RECORD and ACCOUNT-RECORD still correct
recommended_change_sequence.validation: Unit test 4000-PROCESS-PAYMENT; integration test with refactored PAYMENT-HANDLER; verify WS-PAYMENT-REQUEST correctly populated

recommended_change_sequence.description: Integration test the full call chain; verify data flows correctly from customer records through payment processing; test error handling and return codes
recommended_change_sequence.validation: End-to-end integration tests; verify PAYMENT-LOG output; check ACCOUNT-FILE updates; confirm no data loss or corruption

recommended_change_sequence.description: Run full batch cycle with BATCH-RUNNER as entry point; verify batch statistics, error handling, and reporting all work correctly with modified PAYMENT-RECORD
recommended_change_sequence.validation: Full regression test suite; compare batch reports before/after; verify PAYMENT-LOG consistency; check BATCH-REPORT output

recommended_change_sequence.description: Deploy all modified programs (PAYMENT-HANDLER, ACCOUNT-MGR) and new PAYMENT-RECORD copybook to test environment; run parallel testing with production data subset
recommended_change_sequence.validation: Parallel run comparison; verify test environment stability; user acceptance testing

recommended_change_sequence.description: Deploy to production with rollback plan; monitor PAYMENT-LOG output closely; implement phased rollout if possible (e.g., pilot customer segment first)
recommended_change_sequence.validation: Production smoke tests; monitor batch run statistics; verify PAYMENT-LOG format; check for file I/O errors

recommended_change_sequence.description: Monitor production for 2-3 batch cycles; document lessons learned; update technical documentation; archive old PAYMENT-LOG format specification
recommended_change_sequence.validation: No production incidents; batch statistics within normal ranges; PAYMENT-LOG audit checks pass

risk_factors.impact: Control flow bugs could cause payment routing errors or missed logging
risk_factors.mitigation: Refactor GOTO statements BEFORE copybook changes (Step 2)

risk_factors.impact: Financial calculation errors or data loss if precision/scale changes incorrectly
risk_factors.mitigation: Data architect review; decimal alignment verification; extensive financial testing with boundary values

risk_factors.impact: Payment routing logic breaks if PAY-TYPE or PAY-STATUS values change
risk_factors.mitigation: Document all condition name values; test all EVALUATE paths; consider externalizing values to configuration if feasible

risk_factors.impact: Historical audit queries break if file format changes; compliance concerns
risk_factors.mitigation: Maintain parallel logging during transition; create file conversion utilities; document format changes for audit team

risk_factors.impact: Field mapping errors between CUSTOMER-RECORD, ACCOUNT-RECORD, and PAYMENT-RECORD
risk_factors.mitigation: Document all field-to-field mappings; test data flow integration; verify PIC clause compatibility

risk_factors.impact: BATCH-RUNNER orchestrates entire process - failures propagate to full batch run
risk_factors.mitigation: Full regression testing (Step 7); parallel run in test environment; phased production rollout

risk_factors.impact: Caller-callee mismatch between ACCOUNT-MGR and PAYMENT-HANDLER if structure changes incompatibly
risk_factors.mitigation: Coordinated recompilation; verify USING clause parameters match; integration testing (Step 6)

mitigation_strategies.description: Eliminate GOTO statements in PAYMENT-HANDLER BEFORE modifying PAYMENT-RECORD; reduces control flow risk and makes testing more reliable
Write to both old and new PAYMENT-LOG formats during transition period; allows audit trail continuity and rollback capability
Deploy to pilot customer segment first if architecturally feasible; monitor for issues before full rollout
Test PAY-AMOUNT calculations with boundary values (0, negative, maximum, decimal edge cases); verify COMP-3 precision maintained
Document PIC clause compatibility for all field mappings between CUSTOMER-RECORD, ACCOUNT-RECORD, and PAYMENT-RECORD; catch mismatch errors early
Build comprehensive test suite covering all payment types, error conditions, and batch scenarios; run in CI/CD pipeline

Full call chain: CUSTOMER-PROC → ACCOUNT-MGR → PAYMENT-HANDLER
Data flow verification from CUSTOMER-REC to PAYMENT-LOG
Error handling and return codes at each level
ACCOUNT-FILE updates trigger correct payment processing
Complete batch run with production-like data volume
Parallel run comparison (old vs new structure)
Boundary values: 0, negative, maximum, decimal edge cases
End-user scenarios for all payment types

Tests GOTO logic (or refactored equivalent); verify return codes and error handling

cypher_queries_simulated.purpose: Find all programs that directly include PAYMENT-RECORD
cypher_queries_simulated.query: MATCH (p:Program)-[:INCLUDES]->(cb:Copybook {name: 'PAYMENT-RECORD'}) RETURN p.program_id, p.estimated_complexity, p.source_path ORDER BY p.program_id

cypher_queries_simulated.purpose: Find upstream callers (who calls programs that use PAYMENT-RECORD)
cypher_queries_simulated.query: MATCH (cb:Copybook {name: 'PAYMENT-RECORD'})<-[:INCLUDES]-(p:Program) OPTIONAL MATCH path = (upstream:Program)-[:CALLS*1..5]->(p) RETURN upstream.program_id AS caller, length(path) AS depth, collect(DISTINCT p.program_id) AS affects ORDER BY depth

cypher_queries_simulated.purpose: Find downstream callees (what do programs using PAYMENT-RECORD call)
cypher_queries_simulated.query: MATCH (cb:Copybook {name: 'PAYMENT-RECORD'})<-[:INCLUDES]-(p:Program) OPTIONAL MATCH (p)-[:CALLS]->(downstream:Program) RETURN p.program_id AS caller, collect(downstream.program_id) AS calls
cypher_queries_simulated.results_summary: ACCOUNT-MGR calls PAYMENT-HANDLER; PAYMENT-HANDLER calls nothing (leaf program)

cypher_queries_simulated.query: MATCH (p:Program)-[:INCLUDES]->(cb:Copybook {name: 'PAYMENT-RECORD'}) MATCH (p)-[:INCLUDES]->(other:Copybook) WHERE other.name <> 'PAYMENT-RECORD' RETURN p.program_id, collect(other.name) AS other_copybooks
cypher_queries_simulated.results_summary: ACCOUNT-MGR uses CUSTOMER-RECORD and ACCOUNT-RECORD; PAYMENT-HANDLER uses no other copybooks

report_generation_metadata.confidence_level: HIGH - Based on complete parsed data and known graph structure
